source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Time Injury from Fall
actions_taken: Crane operator locked down crane and checked on injured person
Platform personnel assisted injured person across deck
Injured person transported via stokes litter to helideck using crane
Injured person transported to University of Texas Medical Branch (UTMB) Galveston
event.summary: On February 7, 2024, at approximately 09:30 am, a loss time injury occurred when a Danos construction worker attempted to climb an out-of-service scaffolding ladder attached to the ExxonMobil Pipeline Company Motor Control Center/Control Building (ZZZ-006) on the Galveston A 244 JP platform. The ladder, which had been placed out of service on January 1, 2024 with the Self-Retracting Lanyard (SRL) removed and no visual identification tags, shifted when the worker reached the 3rd rung, causing him to jump to the deck and sustain injuries to both legs. The incident resulted from multiple failures including inadequate scaffolding construction, lack of proper out-of-service marking, missing fall protection equipment, inadequate work permits that did not address working at heights, poor communication between operators and contractors, and lack of coordination between multiple contractors on the platform.
recommendations: All operators should review or revisit Safety Alert No. 469 (BSEE Identifies Medical Evacuation and Emergency Hazards During Risk-Based Inspections)
key_phrases: fall arrest harness weight capacity mismatch
single attachment point at top and bottom
working at heights not mentioned in permits

threats.name: Scaffolding ladder structural failure due to inadequate construction and attachment
Use of out-of-service equipment without visual identification
Fall arrest equipment inadequately rated or improperly attached
Worker unaware of working at heights hazard due to inadequate work permits and communication

consequences.name: Loss time injury requiring medical evacuation

controls.name: Scaffolding ladder design and construction standards

supporting_text: The scaffolding ladder being used had several issues as well. It was built and attached to the building with only one attachment point at the top and bottom
The legs of the ladder were not on the same plane as the platform deck, allowing some means to stabilize the ladder from unequal deflection which contributed to its instability
Flawed equipment construction the scaffold ladder anchor points were unconventional & not best practices
No identifying tags or red tape was used to 'mark/identify' the scaffolding ladder being placed OOS
Inadequate out of service tags for equipment there was no other visual identifiers letting platform personnel know this scaffolding ladder was placed OOS
Once the tag and SRL was removed there were no other visual identifiers letting platform personnel know this scaffolding ladder was placed OOS
there was no inspection records available for the harness and associated fall arrest equipment being used
no tags associated with this scaffolding could be presented to investigators showing this ladder was inspected and maintained
Inadequate preventative maintenance has been identified in the maintenance and inspection of built scaffolding on this facility

controls.name: Fall arrest harness and equipment selection and inspection

supporting_text: there was a harness being used with a tag that stated it was only to be used within a certain weight capacity range. Next, the fall arrest equipment that was being used with the harness was rated for a higher weight capacity than the harness and was not attached correctly
Prior to job improper selection of safety equipment

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and work permits addressing working at heights
controls.barrier_role: Identify and communicate working at heights hazards

supporting_text: Each of the permits created did not mention working from heights

It was later discovered that no discussion of working at heights or the requirement for a job hazard analysis was performed because there was not supposed to be any working at heights to be done on the day of the injury
comments made by platform personnel was that no working at heights was discussed during any meetings or work permit approvals

controls.name: Communication and coordination between operator and contractors
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all parties aware of hazards and procedures

supporting_text: The Platform Right of Way is operated by Williams Oil Gathering (Person in Charge (PIC) is a Williams representative) with Exxon personnel onboard the platform. Interestingly, the PIC for the platform was not involved with either Exxon's or Danos's JSA process
there is no bridging document between Exxon and Williams companies
Each JSA was signed only by their respective personnel i.e., Danos personnel on Danos with Danos personnel not on Williams or ExxonMobil other contractor JSA's

controls.name: Self-Retracting Lanyard (SRL) installation and maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Provide fall arrest capability for working at heights

supporting_text: The SRL was removed from the top of the scaffold ladder/work area to be used in another area of the platform, which further exacerbated the ladder's unsafe condition
the scaffold builder removed the SRL and tag at the same time

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting and coordination between all contractors
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all parties understand work scope and hazards

supporting_text: No pre-job safety meeting between all parties were four separate work permits created. One for Williams Pipeline (Work Permit), two for Danos (JSA), and one for EMPCO (JLA). Each contractor did not sign the other contractors JSA or were part of their respective toolbox/safety meeting on the platform

controls.name: Operator approval and oversight of contractor work permits
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operator awareness and approval of all work activities

supporting_text: Inadequate approval of work permits from platform lease operators not having any input into other JSA's created by other contractors

controls.name: Emergency medical response and evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Minimize consequences of injury through rapid medical response

supporting_text: The helicopter arrived two hours after being called
it took over two hours for the IP to be evacuated
The IP was not stabilized on a backboard, neck brace or splint prior to his evacuation from the platform
The SAR times are of serious concern

controls.name: Search and Rescue (SAR) asset availability and response capability

supporting_text: All SAR assets come from Louisiana and the incident occurred off the coast of Texas
Depending on the platform subscribers tier level with SAR companies, the allowed SAR asset varies

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
